LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT STEVEN SMITH'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS AGAINST HIM IS DENIED. PLAINTIFFS ALLEGE THAT SMITH STRUCK THEIR VEHICLE BY RUNNING A RED LIGHT AT A BUSY CITY INTERSECTION WHILE UNDER THE INFLUENCE OF ALCOHOL AND/OR DRUGS. THIS CONSTITUTES "A CONSCIENCE DISREGARD OF THE PROBABLE DANGEROUS CONSEQUENCES" - PARTICULARLY AT THE PLEADING STAGE. (SEE TAYLOR V. SUP. CT. (1979) 24 CAL.3D 890, 892.) PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-09-2022 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-07-2022 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Aug-30-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-17-2021 Set For Trial On Apr-11-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-10-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Lauren Meisel (415-788-2035) of Meisel Law Group for Plaintiff.
Christina Kim (925-446-3115; Christina_Kim @Progressive.com) of Gilsleider, McMahon, Molinelli & Phan for Defendant.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Steven Smith's motion to strike the punitive damages allegations against him is denied. Plaintiffs allege that Smith struck their vehicle by running a red light at a busy city intersection while under the influence of alcohol and/or drugs. This constitutes "a conscience disregard of the probable dangerous consequences" - particularly at the pleading stage. (See Taylor v. Sup. Ct. (1979) 24 Cal.3d 890, 892.)

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
